title: cytisus hirsutus var. supinus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus var. supinus, also known as the hairy broom or Scotch broom, is a shrub-like plant species within the Fabaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cytisus hirsutus var. supinus include its potential to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and aid in the treatment of respiratory problems. It is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, with dried flowers and leaves being used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
